title: Issues running podman on kernel 6.15.3
body:
### Issue Description

I have just upgrade to kernel 6.15.3 and podman started to give so many warnings about cgroup been on v1.
I changed to cgroup v2, than podman started to request `pasta`(`passt`) when it never was installed.
It appears to ignore `slirp4netns` existence.

I am using the latest podman and his deps, which I have built to update the scripts on Slackbuilds.org

Using Slackware64 15.0-current

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Install podman on Slackware64 -current
2. Upgrade the kernel to 6.15.3 from testing
3. Run `podman run busybox` as example
4. Error

### Describe the results you received

```bash
Error: could not find pasta, the network namespace can't be configured: exec: "pasta": executable file not found in $PATH
```

### Describe the results you expected

Been able to use podman without pasta/passt been installed.

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

I am in process to create a VM to with kernel 6.14.11 which was the one used until yesterday.

comments.body: if you want to use slirp4netns, set the value of `default_rootless_network_cmd` in the `containers.conf` file to `slirp4netns`.
